_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

The user wants to change the experience selection logic so that experiences are only excluded if the user has explicitly approved an "Omit" recommendation. Currently, the code uses a whitelist approach (only including experiences that are in `recommended_exp_ids`). We need to flip this to a blacklist approach: include all experiences EXCEPT those explicitly omitted.

Let me look at the data structures more carefully.

The `customizations` dict has:
- `recommended_experiences`: list of IDs (from LLM recommendations)
- `experience_decisions`: dict of {id: decision} from user review decisions (values like 'emphasize', 'include', 'de-emphasize', 'omit')


- `experience_recommendations`: list of dicts with {id, recommendation, confidence, reasoning}

I need to trace through how `customizations` gets populated when `_select_content_hybrid` is called. Looking at the `generate_cv` action in `conversation_manager.py`, it merges user decisions into `customizations['recommended_experiences']` by collecting emphasized and included experiences...

Still processing user decisions...

The key insight is that omitted items are implicitly excluded by not appearing in `recommended_experiences`, but the orchestrator only includes what's explicitly in `recommended_exp_ids`, which means unreviewed experiences also get filtered out. I need to flip this logic: include everything by default and only exclude items the user explicitly marked as omit. The challenge is that by the time `_select_content_hybrid` runs, the customizations dict has already been processed, so I need to track which IDs were explicitly omitted separately.

Storing omitted IDs in customizations...

The user wants a single HTML file that is both ATS-friendly (suitable for direct upload to ATS systems) AND serves as the source for PDF rendering. Currently we have two separate HTML templates:

2. `cv-ats.html` - the ATS-friendly template with JSON-LD and plain text

The user wants to merge these into one: a single HTML file that:
- Looks good for direct viewing/uploading
- Has ATS features (JSON-LD, plain text hidden section)
- Can be rendered to PDF via WeasyPrint

So I'll merge the ATS features into the main template, then simplify the generation logic to work with just one HTML file instead of maintaining two separate paths.
